criterion_ref: Signature check runs before body-shape validation: an invalid body with a valid signature is `400`; any body (valid or invalid) with an invalid signature is `401`.
message: `.update(req.body)` assumes the raw middleware always supplies a Buffer. In-process dispatch of the actual route with no Content-Length/Transfer-Encoding left `req.body` as `{}` and raised `ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE` for both a valid empty-body HMAC and an invalid signature, instead of returning 400 and 401 respectively.

criterion_ref: Signature check runs before body-shape validation: an invalid body with a valid signature is `400`; any body (valid or invalid) with an invalid signature is `401`.
message: `express.raw({ type: '*/*' })` applies Express's default 100kb limit before the handler can verify the signature. Actual route dispatch with 102401 bytes and an invalid signature produced `PayloadTooLargeError` status 413, not the required 401, so signature verification is not unconditional for every body.

criterion_ref: HMAC must be computed over the exact raw bytes of the request body as received, not a re-serialization of parsed JSON.
message: `express.raw` defaults to `inflate: true`, so `req.body` is decompressed before line 39 computes the HMAC. An actual gzip-encoded request signed over its exact 81 received bytes was inflated to 65 bytes and rejected with 401, demonstrating that the implementation hashes a transformed body rather than the literal received bytes.

criterion_ref: Add POST /webhook ... verifies an X-Signature header ... Mismatch (or malformed/missing header) responds 401
message: POST /webhook with no Content-Type header (and no body) leaves req.body as body-parser's placeholder {} (express.raw's shouldParse gate never matches when there is no Content-Type to test against '*/*'). crypto.createHmac(...).update({}) then throws TypeError [ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE] uncaught inside the route handler, which Express converts to HTTP 500 with a raw Node stack trace (absolute file paths, dependency internals) in the response body -- not any of the spec's defined responses (200/400/401/409), and leaks implementation details to an unauthenticated caller.
